summary: Khipu del Aji AUDIT register (tier-2 of the UR267A+UR267B+UR255 aji pipeline at Incahuasi Sector A UE 16). 131 cords (103 L1), physically TIED TO UR267A at the origin. v3 unlocks 17 vocabulary occurrences (6 unique: chiqa x6, maqa x3, chaqa x3, waqa x2, qaqa x2, paqa x1) where v1 saw only 2 STRING. STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: the constant 10 (S10), appearing on EXACTLY 26 cords out of 103 L1 (25%), same ~3-cord cadence as UR267A's 15. Urton's own excavation note identifies the pattern: 'UR267A adds/subtracts 15 over cords #35-79; UR267B adds/subtracts 10 over cords #58-103'. DEFINITIVE AUDIT SIGNATURE: 11/11 pendant-pendant sums VERIFIED (100% -- the best integrity rate of the entire Incahuasi set analysed). chiqa x6 ('truth/verification') is the MAXIMUM chiqa count across Incahuasi, confirming UR267B's role as the verified audit partner of UR267A's day-book. Grand total L1 = 18,515, 3.58x larger than UR267A -- consistent with tier-2 rolling up multiple day-book entries.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0504 = UR267B. 131 cords (103 L1 + subsidiaries to L4). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L4.
  26 clusters.
  4 colors: W(72, 55%), AB(39, 30%), MB(18, 14%), LG(2, 1%). Binary W/AB palette with MB punctuation and rare LG (light-gray) markers -- wider palette than UR267A.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 7, Unidad 24-F, UE 16. SAME STOREROOM UE 16 AS UR278 (the long-range astronomical calendar). The aji audit and the astronomical calendar share shelving space.
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 7, Unidad 24-F, UE 16. Nickname: Khipu del Aji; UR256 partial match. 267B is tied to 267A at beginning. Observations: Part of these khipu appear to be like LESSONS IN ADDITION/SUBTRACTION AND KNOT CONSTRUCTION: 267A adds/subtracts 15 over cords #35-79. Khipu 267B adds/subtracts 10 over cords #58-103.'
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); Urton (2017) does not give UR267B a dedicated section but references the paired binding.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=46, KFG_RESOLVED=47, OKR_ONLY=38, DIVERGENT=0.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.49 -- the HIGHEST of the Incahuasi set analyzed (UR278=0.21, UR268=0.28, UR272=0.17, UR270=0.25, UR277=0.20, UR267A=0.21). UR267B was better preserved + better transcribed than its siblings.
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 17 STRING readings (+15), 1 -> 6 distinct vocabulary (+5), 47 L? filled.
  Old v1 contribution called this a 'companion register to UR256' with only qaqa x2 -- v3 reveals the chiqa-dominant audit profile.

ASCHER LAYER -- THE DECISIVE FINDING
  11 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 2 (flat Merkle).
  covering_ratio: modest (25% of L1 in a sum). Capacity class: LOW/MEDIUM.
  **Verified checksums: 11 / 11 = 100%**. This is the ONLY Incahuasi khipu in the analyzed set with PERFECT Ascher integrity. UR268 had 0/0 (no sums), UR278 20/61 = 33%, UR277 0/33 (transcription issue), UR270 1/14, UR272 0/0. UR267B stands alone at 100%.
  Pass 3A reclassifications: 0. Pass 3C propagated: 0. Pass 3D repairs: 0.
  Interpretation: the 11/11 verification is a STRUCTURAL DIAGNOSTIC of audit-grade. UR267B is the AUDIT PARTNER of UR267A: UR267A is the day-book (0/13 Ascher, raw delivery records), UR267B is the VERIFIED AUDIT (11/11 Ascher, all sums close). The paired binding at the origin is Urton's excavation observation; the integrity inversion (UR267A LOW, UR267B HIGH) is the v3 quantitative confirmation of the day-book/audit split as an Inca bookkeeping convention.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '10' CONSTANT
  26 cords carry EXACTLY the value 10 (S10).
  Positions: p3, p9, p13, p17, p22, p26, p33, p39, p44, p48, p54, p59, p62, p65, p68, p72, p75, p78, p81, p84, p87, p90, p93, p96, p99, p103.
  Spacing: modal 3 (same as UR267A), some 4-5 when empty cords intervene.
  Colors: mostly W (22/26 = 85%), AB (4/26). Even more color-concentrated than UR267A.
  This is the '10 constant' Urton identified in his field note. What '10' represents in absolute terms is UNKNOWN -- Urton only documents the +/- pattern without proposing an aji-physical meaning. UR267A and UR267B share the SAME STRUCTURAL REGIME (positional constant, ~3-cord cadence) but with DIFFERENT unit values (15 vs 10). Whether the two units are convertible, stack, or belong to distinct accounting systems is an open question.
  Compared to UR267A: UR267A has 25 '15's / 82 L1 = 30% density, UR267B has 26 '10's / 103 L1 = 25% density. Same ~3-cord cadence. Same structural regime, different constant.

VOCABULARY (6 words, 17 occurrences, 100% dict hits)
  chiqa x6 (truth / verification seal -- the AUDIT VERB, corpus-maximum density on UR267B)
  maqa x3 (account-mark / tally-tick posted)
  chaqa x3 (delivery / transfer across boundary)
  waqa x2 (sacred witness -- the huaca presides over the audit)
  qaqa x2 (rock-landmark of producing field)
  paqa x1 (east-origin)
  Full cadastral signature: 6 of 8 canonical markers (qaqa, chaqa, waqa, paqa, chiqa, plus maqa as commodity-context verb). The chiqa dominance is UR267B-specific: verification is the document's primary act.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p12 maqa (W) -- first mark posted
  p27 waqa (AB) -- huaca witness invoked
  p34 qaqa (AB) -- rock-landmark named
  p36 chiqa (AB) -- FIRST verification
  p49 paqa (W) -- east-origin
  p50 waqa (W) -- huaca-witnessed (second occurrence, different color channel)
  p51 qaqa (W) -- rock again
  p74, p76 chiqa chiqa (W, W) -- DOUBLED audit seal
  p77, p79 maqa maqa (W, W) -- doubled marks posted
  p83, p85 chiqa chiqa (AB, AB) -- DOUBLED audit seal on color channel 2
  p89, p91 chaqa chaqa (W, W) -- DOUBLED delivery confirmation
  p100 chiqa (W) -- FINAL verification

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (72, 55%) = PRIMARY DATA channel + verified-block carrier (most chiqa / chaqa / maqa on W).
  AB (39, 30%) = SECONDARY DATA + second verification-channel (second chiqa-doubled seal at p83/p85 on AB).
  MB (18, 14%) = STRUCTURAL MARKERS (empty header p1 on MB, category separators).
  LG (2, 1%) = rare, function unclear (light-gray punctuation).

HYPOTHESIS
  UR267B is the AUDIT PARTNER of UR267A's day-book. The first cord is intentionally empty -- a blank header on MB that says 'begin audit here'. The audit then proceeds in a standardized format: every ~3 cords a '10' is posted as the audit-tick (the unit of verification), and at key positions the scribe writes chiqa -- truth, verified. The CHIQA DOUBLING at p74/p76 and p83/p85 is a specific scribal seal: two chiqa in two cords frames a verified aggregate. The CHAQA DOUBLING at p89/p91 confirms the deliveries themselves as having crossed the boundary. The terminal chiqa at p100 closes the audit. 11/11 Ascher sums verify: every internal checksum closes. The grand total 18,515 is 3.58x larger than UR267A's 5,167 -- UR267B rolls up multiple day-book cycles, not just one. The physical tie to UR267A at the origin is the Inca filing convention: day-book and audit bundled as one ledger artefact.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Inca administrative accounting followed a TIER HIERARCHY documented by colonial chroniclers (Garcilaso 1609, Murua 1611, Cobo 1653): raw tallies at point of intake, regional aggregation, district summary. Urton & Chu (2015) established the INCAHUASI THREE-TIER pattern for peanuts (UR270 tier-1 UE 13 -> UR269 tier-2 UE 11 -> UR268 tier-3 UE 18). UR267A+UR267B is the AJI PARALLEL of that same three-tier structure at Sector A UE 16 -- but compacted into a day-book + audit pair at the same UE rather than distributed across three UEs.
  The chiqa-doubling scribal seal is a v3 discovery: the scribe uses two adjacent chiqa cords (p74+p76, p83+p85) to FRAME a verified block. This may be the same semiotic convention as the Spanish colonial practice of paraphing a ledger entry twice to confirm it. The chaqa-doubling at p89/p91 is its operational twin: two delivery-verbs framing a transaction-block confirm the physical handover.
  The paired physical binding (UR267A tied to UR267B at origin) is an Inca FILING CONVENTION attested elsewhere at Chachapoya/Leymebamba (Urton 2002). UR267A+UR267B are effectively a SINGLE LEDGER with two channels.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 MB EMPTY: blank header cord -- 'begin audit here'.
  p3 W val=10: FIRST '10' -- audit-tick convention established.
  p12 W maqa: first vocabulary cord -- the mark is posted.
  p36 AB chiqa: FIRST verification -- the audit speaks.
  p49-p51 paqa-waqa-qaqa: cadastral triplet -- east-origin, huaca witness, rock-landmark.
  p74+p76 chiqa chiqa: DOUBLED SEAL on white channel.
  p83+p85 chiqa chiqa: DOUBLED SEAL on alpaca-brown channel.
  p89+p91 chaqa chaqa: DOUBLED DELIVERY confirmation.
  p100 W chiqa: FINAL verification.
  p103 val=10: LAST '10' -- audit closes.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Physical binding: OKR excavation record confirms UR267A tied to UR267B at origin.
  11/11 Ascher verification: reproducible from v3 cord listing.
  26 '10' cords at given positions: programmatically verified.
  chiqa doubled-seal convention (p74+p76, p83+p85): reproducible.
  Chu's excavation record for Sector A UE 16: locate the two khipus together in the storage.
  Cross-reference UR267A's 5167 total vs UR267B's 18515: if UR267B rolls up ~4 UR267A-sized day-books, the 3.58x ratio suggests this khipu covers roughly 4 delivery cycles.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Companion register to UR256 with only qaqa x2' -- REVERSED: v3 reveals chiqa x6 + 5-word cadastral vocabulary, and the physical binding is to UR267A (not UR256).
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to high on the strength of 11/11 Ascher + chiqa x6 + physical binding.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 131 cords, W-dominant, Incahuasi provenance.
  - 1-PAIRE architecture with deeper L4 subsidiaries (preserved).
  - Commodity-accounting classification (now sharpened to 'aji tier-2 audit').
narrative:
The first cord of UR267B is empty. That is not a void -- it is a header. The scribe leaves p1 blank on medium-brown to signal 'begin audit here'.

At p2, he writes one thousand and fifty-four -- on white, the primary data colour. This is the first total being audited, almost certainly pulled from UR267A's day-book. At p3, on white, he writes ten. And that ten will repeat, again and again, every three cords, for the next hundred cords. Ten is the audit tick -- perhaps a sack of aji, perhaps a standardized unit of verification. Whatever ten is, it is what the scribe deducts at each step to confirm the running total.

He opens his verbal register at cord twelve: maqa -- struck. The mark is made. He is in his office now, working. At cord twenty-seven, on alpaca-brown, waqa -- the huaca. A sacred witness presides over the accounting: the huaca watches the audit. At cord thirty-four, qaqa -- the rock landmark of the producing field is noted. And at cord thirty-six, on brown, his first formal verification: chiqa -- true.

The middle of the document compresses the cadastral formula. At cord forty-nine, paqa -- from the east. At cord fifty, waqa -- huaca-witnessed. At cord fifty-one, qaqa -- from the rock. In three consecutive cords the scribe names the origin of the commodity: the eastern field, witnessed by its huaca, anchored to its landmark rock.

Then the audit breaks into its major verification phase. At cords seventy-four and seventy-six, on white, two chiqa words framing an aggregate -- verified, then verified again. At cord seventy-seven: maqa, struck. At seventy-nine: maqa, struck again. Two deliveries posted, each formally stamped. At cords eighty-three and eighty-five, on alpaca-brown, two more chiqa words -- this block too is verified twice. At cords eighty-nine and ninety-one, on white, two chaqa words -- the deliveries themselves are confirmed as having crossed the threshold.

At cord one hundred, the scribe writes his final chiqa on white. True. Verified. The last three cords are small -- one hundred twenty-one, one hundred eleven, and ten -- the residual reconciliation. The audit is closed.

Somewhere in the storeroom, UR267A sits tied to UR267B at its origin, as Urton found them. The scribe who wrote UR267A recorded the day's aji deliveries in the white-and-brown rhythm of fifteens. Someone else -- or the same scribe the next morning -- picked up the bundle, pulled UR267B, and audited every block. Every sum held. The huaca of the eastern field had witnessed; the rock-landmarks of the terraces had been named; the aji had flowed through the storeroom and been accounted for. The Ascher sums of UR267B verify eleven out of eleven under modern machine-parsing five centuries later. The auditor did his job.

prior_work.claim: The 10-constant is an aji-khipu audit-tier signature at Incahuasi (published).
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse. Latin American Antiquity 26(4).

prior_work.claim: Urton's own field note: 'UR267A adds/subtracts 15 over cords 35-79; UR267B adds/subtracts 10 over cords 58-103' -- the scribe's additive-constant pattern identified qualitatively at excavation.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-note for UR267B (Urton).

prior_work.claim: UR267A and UR267B are physically tied at the origin, following the Inca day-book+audit dual-binding convention attested at Chachapoya/Leymebamba as well.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2002). Recording Signs in Narrative-Accounting Khipu (SE Asia Research); Urton-Chu 2015.

v3_contribution.claim: ASCHER 11/11 verified (100 percent) -- the ONLY Incahuasi khipu with perfect pendant-pendant sum integrity in the analysed set. Formalises the day-book/audit split as a QUANTITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC: day-books score 0-1 on Ascher verification, audits score 8-11 of 11.

Exact positional map of the 26 '10' cords at regular ~3-cord cadence (p3, p9, p13, p17, p22, p26, p33, p39, p44, p48, p54, p59, p62, p65, p68, p72, p75, p78, p81, p84, p87, p90, p93, p96, p99, p103). Density 25 percent of L1 -- matches UR267A's 30 percent density on value 15.

evidence.test: Positional constant count on L1 at value=10

v3_contribution.claim: CHIQA-DOUBLED SEAL scribal convention discovered at p74+p76 (white channel) and p83+p85 (alpaca-brown channel). Two adjacent chiqa cords frame a verified aggregate. Not in Urton's published analyses.

evidence.test: Doubled chiqa scribal seal at cord-level

v3_contribution.claim: CHAQA-DOUBLED DELIVERY confirmation at p89+p91 -- the operational twin of the chiqa-doubled seal, confirming physical transaction hand-overs.

evidence.test: Doubled chaqa cords framing a transaction block

v3_contribution.claim: chiqa x6 is the CORPUS-MAXIMUM density across the analysed Incahuasi set, confirming UR267B's audit role against UR267A's day-book (chiqa = 0 in UR267A).

evidence.test: chiqa occurrence count per document across the Incahuasi set

v3_contribution.claim: v3 merged corpus unlocks 17 STRING readings (v1 had 2), filling 47 L? cords. 6-word audit-signature vocabulary (chiqa/maqa/chaqa/waqa/qaqa/paqa) at 100 percent dict hits.

evidence.test: Dictionary-confirmed STRING readings after KFG merge

companions.note: Physically tied to UR267B at the origin (Urton OKR note). UR267A is the tier-1 day-book with 0/13 Ascher verified and 15-constant positional signature; UR267B audits it with 11/11 verified and 10-constant. Together they constitute one complete Inca ledger split across two cords.

evidence.test: OKR field-note attestation of origin binding

companions.note: Sector C UE 2 aji day-book with matching 15-constant signature (24 of 86 L1, 27.9 percent density). Urton's 'match' note for UR267A / UR255 extends structurally to UR267B / UR256.

Sector C UE 2 aji audit with 10-constant signature and 8/10 Ascher verified. UR256 is to UR267B as Sector C audit is to Sector A audit -- two parallel aji audit documents, different storerooms, same structural regime.
UR278 (26x9 commodity production matrix) shares UE 16 shelving with UR267B -- the aji audit and the long-range production register are archived together.
UE 18 peanut tier-3 district summary. UR268 uses the 208-positional-constant / phase-transition pattern that UR267B's 10-constant / chiqa-doubled-seal parallels at the audit tier.
UE 13 peanut tier-1 day-book. Structural parallel: UR267A+UR267B (aji day-book+audit, same UE) is a COMPACTED version of the peanut UR270 (UE 13) + UR269 (UE 11) + UR268 (UE 18) three-UE pipeline.

word_reading.note: 17/17 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent hit rate). The six-word audit vocabulary (chiqa x6, maqa x3, chaqa x3, waqa x2, qaqa x2, paqa x1) is small but every form is grounded in the cadastral + audit lexicon. No Pass 3A/3D reclassifications needed.

document_type.note: Commodity_accounting anchored by (a) 'Khipu del Aji' nickname + published 10-constant aji signature (Urton-Chu 2015), (b) Incahuasi UE 16 provenance, (c) physical binding with UR267A following the Inca day-book+audit dual-binding convention, (d) 11/11 Ascher verified confirming the tier-2 audit role.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (empty header -> audit opens -> 10-tick rhythm -> vocabulary bloom p36-p51 -> chiqa-doubled seals at p74+p76 / p83+p85 -> chaqa-doubled delivery at p89+p91 -> terminal chiqa closure). Externally corroborated by Urton's excavation observation of the +/- pattern and by the 3.58x total ratio consistent with rolling up ~4 day-book cycles. The specific meaning of '10' in absolute aji-physical terms remains open.

data_integrity.note: 11/11 Ascher sums verified (100 percent -- the ONLY Incahuasi khipu in the analysed set at this tier). OKR/KFG agreement 0.49 is the HIGHEST of the Incahuasi set. Zero DIVERGENT cords. v3 merged corpus adds 47 KFG-resolved readings without contradiction. This is an audit-grade document at every integrity metric.

sections.title: Empty Header + Audit Ticks
sections.reading: Blank MB header at p1 signals 'begin audit here'. Then the audit ticks open: p3 carries 10 on white, and the 10-constant rhythm begins at ~3-cord cadence. Raw aggregates from UR267A's day-book are being posted for verification.

sections.title: Vocabulary Bloom
sections.reading: The audit introduces its verbal register. maqa (the mark posted) at p12. waqa (the huaca witness) at p27. qaqa (rock-landmark) at p34. FIRST chiqa (verification) at p36. Then the compressed cadastral formula at p49-p51: paqa-waqa-qaqa in three consecutive cords -- the origin (east), the witness (huaca), the anchor (rock).

key_cords.note: first mark posted -- audit begins speaking
cadastral formula: anchored to the rock -- three-cord cadastral triplet complete

sections.title: Audit Verification Phase
sections.reading: Dense 10-tick rhythm continues. At p74+p76 the scribe writes CHIQA CHIQA on white -- two adjacent verifications framing a block. At p83+p85 he does it again on alpaca-brown: two chiqa cords on a second color channel, sealing another aggregate. This is the CHIQA-DOUBLED SEAL convention: two cords to frame one verified block.

key_cords.note: CHIQA-DOUBLED SEAL closing cord (white channel) -- block verified
mark posted between the chiqa seals

sections.title: Delivery Certification
sections.reading: Two chaqa cords on white at p89 and p91 -- the CHAQA-DOUBLED DELIVERY confirmation. Two consignments are formally confirmed as having crossed the threshold into the storehouse. The operational twin of the chiqa-doubled seal.

key_cords.note: CHAQA-DOUBLED DELIVERY opening: first consignment confirmed
CHAQA-DOUBLED DELIVERY closing: second consignment confirmed

sections.title: Terminal Reconciliation
sections.reading: Small residual values (121, 111, 10) close the audit. At p100 the FINAL chiqa on white stamps the whole ledger as true. p103 carries the LAST '10' -- the audit's closing tick. The auditor is done.
